subject: First Year Topology
instructions: For problems 1–7, complete proofs. Support all statements to the best of your ability. For problems 8–13, answer the following with complete definitions or statements or short proofs.

problems.text: Suppose that the continuous maps \(f,g:X\to Y\) are homotopic and the continuous maps \(k,h:Y\to Z\) are homotopic. Prove that \(k\circ f\) and \(h\circ g\) are homotopic.
Prove that in a simply connected space \(X\), any two paths having the same initial and final points are path homotopic.

Prove that if \(X\) is path connected and \(x_0\) and \(x_1\) are two points of \(X\), then \(\pi_1(X,x_0)\) is isomorphic to \(\pi_1(X,x_1)\). Recall that \(\pi_1(X,x_0)\) denotes the fundamental group of \(X\) relative to the base point \(x_0\).
Let \(p:E\to B\) be a covering map, and let \(p(e_0)=b_0\). Let \(f:[0,1]\to B\) be a path beginning at \(b_0\). Prove that \(f\) has a lifting \(\widetilde f\) beginning at \(e_0\).
Prove that there is no continuous antipode-preserving map \(g:S^2\to S^1\).
Prove that if \(n\geq 2\), then \(S^n\) is simply connected.
Prove that the fundamental group of the projective plane \(P^2\) is a group of order 2.
Complete the following definition: Two spaces \(X\) and \(Y\) have the same homotopy type if and only if . . . .
Is the identity map \(i:S^1\to S^1\) nullhomotopic?
What is the fundamental group of the torus \(T=S^1\times S^1\)?
Complete the following definition: Let \(X\) be a topological space and let \(A\) be a subspace of \(X\). We say that \(A\) is a retract of \(X\) if and only if . . . .
